seo.title: Pydantic AI Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Pydantic AI agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams adopting Pydantic AI for document review, case analysis, and contract drafting face heightened risks due to strict ethical obligations and regulatory scrutiny. Hallucinated citations or breaches of attorney-client privilege can lead to malpractice suits, bar sanctions, or case dismissals, as seen in the Mata v. Avianca precedent. Vex provides essential compliance guardrails by validating structured AI outputs against legal standards before they reach production workflows.

complianceRequirements.description: AI systems must not expose or infer confidential client communications without explicit safeguards.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex result validators scan outputs for PII, privilege keywords, and context leaks, blocking non-compliant responses.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content requires human attorney review and must not mislead courts or clients.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags hallucinated citations and unverified case law, enforcing 'attorney-reviewed' metadata in outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: All filings must include verifiable citations to real cases with accurate docket numbers and jurisdictions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex cross-references citations against legal databases, rejecting outputs with fabricated or outdated references.

complianceRequirements.description: AI must preserve chain-of-custody for document relevance scoring and privilege logging.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits structured eDiscovery outputs for complete metadata trails and privilege assertions.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: AI fabricates non-existent cases, leading to court sanctions (Mata v. Avianca, 2023).
industryRisks.example: Pydantic AI outputs 'Smith v. Jones, 2024' which doesn't exist, filed in motion.

industryRisks.name: Privilege data leakage
industryRisks.description: Client confidential info appears in AI-generated summaries or responses.
industryRisks.example: Contract review tool exposes negotiation strategies in public memo draft.

industryRisks.name: Unreviewed AI content in filings
industryRisks.description: AI-drafted briefs filed without attorney verification violate ethics rules.
industryRisks.example: Motion to dismiss contains AI errors missed in rushed review process.

industryRisks.name: eDiscovery chain-of-custody breaks
industryRisks.description: AI relevance scores lack audit trails, tainting evidence admissibility.
industryRisks.example: Privilege log generated by AI omits key metadata fields.

implementation.title: Install Vex and Configure Result Validator
implementation.description: Add Vex as a Pydantic AI result_validator to catch privilege leaks and citation errors at runtime.
implementation.code:
import os

from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex import Vex

# Initialize Vex with legal compliance policy
vex = Vex(api_key=os.getenv('VEX_API_KEY'))

@vex.result_validator(policy='legal-compliance')
def legal_content_validator(output: dict) -> bool:
    # Check for privilege keywords
    privilege_terms = ['confidential', 'attorney-client', 'settlement']
    if any(term in str(output).lower() for term in privilege_terms):
        return False
    # Verify citations exist
    citations = output.get('citations', [])
    for citation in citations:
        if not vex.validate_legal_citation(citation):
            return False
    return True

# Apply to Pydantic AI agent
case_analyzer = Agent(
    model='gpt-4o',
    result_validator=legal_content_validator
)

implementation.title: Validate Structured Legal Outputs
implementation.description: Ensure citation verification and attorney review flags in case brief generation.
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List

from pydantic_ai import Agent

class LegalBrief(BaseModel):
    summary: str
    citations: List[str] = Field(..., description='Verified case citations')
    attorney_reviewed: bool = Field(default=False)

@vex.result_validator(policy='court-filing')
def court_filing_validator(brief: LegalBrief) -> bool:
    # Enforce attorney review for filings
    if not brief.attorney_reviewed:
        raise ValueError('Attorney review required for court filings')
    # Validate all citations
    for citation in brief.citations:
        if not vex.check_case_exists(citation):
            raise ValueError(f'Invalid citation: {citation}')
    return True

brief_agent = Agent(
    model='gpt-4o',
    output_type=LegalBrief,
    result_validator=court_filing_validator
)

result = brief_agent.run('Analyze contract dispute')
print(result)

implementation.title: eDiscovery Privilege Scanner
implementation.description: Scan documents for privilege in structured eDiscovery workflows with retry logic.
implementation.code:
class DiscoveryDocument(BaseModel):
    content: str
    privilege_score: float
    privilege_log: str
    metadata: dict

@vex.result_validator(policy='ediscovery')
def ediscovery_validator(doc: DiscoveryDocument) -> bool:
    # Block high-privilege docs without log
    if doc.privilege_score > 0.8 and not doc.privilege_log:
        return False
    # Verify chain-of-custody metadata
    required_fields = ['hash', 'timestamp', 'custodian']
    if not all(field in doc.metadata for field in required_fields):
        return False
    return True

ediscovery_agent = Agent(
    model='gpt-4o',
    output_type=DiscoveryDocument,
    result_validator=ediscovery_validator,
    max_retries=3
)

docs = ediscovery_agent.run_batch([
    'Review contract for privilege',
    'Analyze email chain'
])

caseStudyScenario.heading: Pydantic AI in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized litigation firm deployed Pydantic AI agents for accelerating eDiscovery in a class-action lawsuit involving 500,000 documents. The agents categorized documents by relevance, flagged potential privilege, and drafted initial privilege logs using structured Pydantic models. Without compliance guardrails, the system began surfacing documents with high privilege scores but incomplete metadata, risking waiver of attorney-client privilege across the entire dataset.

Additionally, the case analysis agent generated motion drafts containing citations to non-existent cases, which junior associates incorporated into filings without verification. During a critical discovery deadline, the firm faced a motion to compel when opposing counsel identified fabricated citations from the AI-generated privilege log. The lack of audit trails for AI relevance scoring further complicated defensibility of their production.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex result validators on all Pydantic AI agents, the firm achieved 100% compliance with eDiscovery protocols. Vex blocked 17% of outputs containing privilege leaks or invalid citations, forcing human review and preventing waiver. The citation validator caught all hallucinated cases by cross-referencing against Westlaw, saving the firm from court sanctions. Post-implementation, their privilege log passed opposing counsel's scrutiny, and the case settled favorably with full defensibility of their AI-assisted production.
